---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Jovin Lasway"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
rm(list = ls()); gc()

library(arrow)

master_data <- read_parquet(
  "C:/Users/laswa/Downloads/master_data_with_travel_cost.parquet"
)

# Check it loaded
dim(master_data)
names(master_data)
head(master_data)
```


```{r cars}
library(arrow)
library(dplyr)

master_ds <- open_dataset(
  "C:/Users/laswa/Downloads/master_data_with_travel_cost.parquet"
)

# check columns
names(master_ds)

# see first rows without loading everything
master_ds %>%
  head(10) %>%
  collect()

# load only selected columns
master_small <- master_ds %>%
  select(species, season, travel_cost, everything()) %>%
  collect()
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r}
library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

master_ds <- open_dataset(
  "C:/Users/laswa/Downloads/master_data_with_travel_cost.parquet"
)

monthly_demand <- master_ds %>%
  mutate(
    month_date = as.Date(paste0(year_month, "-01"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(month_date) %>%
  summarise(
    total_visits = n(),
    migrant_richness = mean(migrant_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    resident_richness = mean(resident_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  collect()

ggplot(monthly_demand, aes(x = month_date, y = total_visits)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "User Visits by Month Over Time",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of User Visits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# Open full parquet without loading it into memory
master_ds <- open_dataset(
  "C:/Users/laswa/Downloads/master_data_with_travel_cost.parquet"
)

# Summarize full data by month first
monthly_demand <- master_ds %>%
  group_by(year_month) %>%
  summarise(
    total_visits = n(),
    migrant_visits = sum(migrant_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    resident_visits = sum(resident_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_migrant_richness = mean(migrant_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_resident_richness = mean(resident_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(
    month_date = as.Date(paste0(as.character(year_month), "-01"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(month_date)

# Graph 1: total visits over time
ggplot(monthly_demand, aes(x = month_date, y = total_visits)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "User Visits by Month Over Time",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of User Visits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Graph 2: migrant vs resident visits
monthly_visits_long <- monthly_demand %>%
  select(month_date, migrant_visits, resident_visits) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(migrant_visits, resident_visits),
    names_to = "bird_type",
    values_to = "visits"
  )

ggplot(monthly_visits_long, aes(x = month_date, y = visits, color = bird_type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "User Visits by Month: Migrant vs Resident Birds",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of User Visits",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
rm(list = ls())
gc()

library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Open full parquet file
# ------------------------------------------------------------

master_ds <- open_dataset(
  "C:/Users/laswa/Downloads/master_data_with_travel_cost.parquet"
)
names (master_ds)

# First 50 rows
master_50 <- master_ds %>%
  head(50) %>%
  collect()

View(master_50)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Seasonal demand: full data summarized by year-season
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_demand <- master_ds %>%
  filter(!is.na(year), !is.na(season)) %>%
  group_by(year, season) %>%
  summarise(
    total_visits = n(),
    migrant_visits = sum(migrant_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    resident_visits = sum(resident_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_migrant_richness = mean(migrant_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_resident_richness = mean(resident_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(
    season = factor(
      season,
      levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Fall")
    ),
    year_season = paste(year, season, sep = "-")
  ) %>%
  arrange(year, season)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Figure 1: total visits by season over time
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot(seasonal_demand,
       aes(x = year_season, y = total_visits, group = 1)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Birdwatching Demand by Season Over Time",
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Number of User Visits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Figure 2: migrant vs resident visits by season over time
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_visits_long <- seasonal_demand %>%
  select(year, season, year_season, migrant_visits, resident_visits) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(migrant_visits, resident_visits),
    names_to = "bird_type",
    values_to = "visits"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    bird_type = recode(
      bird_type,
      migrant_visits = "Migrant",
      resident_visits = "Resident"
    )
  )

ggplot(seasonal_visits_long,
       aes(x = year_season, y = visits,
           color = bird_type, group = bird_type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "User Visits by Season: Migrant vs Resident Birds",
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Number of User Visits",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Cleaner paper-style figure: average seasonal demand
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_avg <- master_ds %>%
  filter(!is.na(season)) %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(
    total_visits = n(),
    migrant_visits = sum(migrant_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    resident_visits = sum(resident_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_migrant_richness = mean(migrant_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_resident_richness = mean(resident_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(
    season = factor(
      season,
      levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Fall")
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(season)

seasonal_avg_long <- seasonal_avg %>%
  select(season, migrant_visits, resident_visits) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(migrant_visits, resident_visits),
    names_to = "bird_type",
    values_to = "visits"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    bird_type = recode(
      bird_type,
      migrant_visits = "Migrant",
      resident_visits = "Resident"
    )
  )

ggplot(seasonal_visits_long,
       aes(x = year_season, y = visits,
           color = bird_type, group = bird_type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = seasonal_demand$year_season[
      seasonal_demand$year %in% c(2015, 2017, 2019, 2021)
    ]
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "User Visits by Season: Migrant vs Resident Birds",
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Number of User Visits",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```


```{r}
rm(list = ls())
gc()

library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Open full parquet file
# ------------------------------------------------------------

master_ds <- open_dataset(
  "C:/Users/laswa/Downloads/master_data_with_travel_cost.parquet"
)

names(master_ds)

# First 50 rows
master_50 <- master_ds %>%
  head(50) %>%
  collect()

View(master_50)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Seasonal demand: full data summarized by year-season
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_demand <- master_ds %>%
  filter(!is.na(year), !is.na(season)) %>%
  group_by(year, season) %>%
  summarise(
    total_visits = n(),
    migrant_visits = sum(migrant_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    resident_visits = sum(resident_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_migrant_richness = mean(migrant_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_resident_richness = mean(resident_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(
    season = factor(
      season,
      levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Fall")
    ),
    year_season = paste(year, season, sep = "-")
  ) %>%
  arrange(year, season)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Figure 1: total visits by season over time
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot(seasonal_demand,
       aes(x = year_season, y = total_visits, group = 1)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Number of User Visits"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Figure 2: migrant vs resident visits by season over time
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_visits_long <- seasonal_demand %>%
  select(year, season, year_season, migrant_visits, resident_visits) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(migrant_visits, resident_visits),
    names_to = "bird_type",
    values_to = "visits"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    bird_type = recode(
      bird_type,
      migrant_visits = "Migrant",
      resident_visits = "Resident"
    )
  )

ggplot(seasonal_visits_long,
       aes(x = year_season, y = visits,
           color = bird_type, group = bird_type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Number of User Visits",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Paper-style figure with Total + Migrant + Resident
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_avg <- master_ds %>%
  filter(!is.na(season)) %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(
    total_visits = n(),
    migrant_visits = sum(migrant_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    resident_visits = sum(resident_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_migrant_richness = mean(migrant_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_resident_richness = mean(resident_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(
    season = factor(
      season,
      levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Fall")
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(season)

seasonal_visits_long <- seasonal_demand %>%
  select(year, season, year_season, migrant_visits, resident_visits) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(migrant_visits, resident_visits),
    names_to = "bird_type",
    values_to = "visits"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    bird_type = recode(
      bird_type,
      migrant_visits = "Migrant",
      resident_visits = "Resident"
    )
  )

# Add total visits
total_visits_long <- seasonal_demand %>%
  select(year, season, year_season, total_visits) %>%
  rename(visits = total_visits) %>%
  mutate(bird_type = "Total")

seasonal_visits_long <- bind_rows(
  seasonal_visits_long,
  total_visits_long
)

ggplot(seasonal_visits_long,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = visits,
           color = bird_type,
           group = bird_type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = seasonal_demand$year_season[
      seasonal_demand$year %in% c(2015, 2017, 2019, 2021)
    ]
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Number of User Visits",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Difference between migrant and resident visits
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_difference <- seasonal_demand %>%
  mutate(
    migrant_resident_diff = migrant_visits - resident_visits
  )

ggplot(seasonal_difference,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = migrant_resident_diff,
           group = 1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = seasonal_demand$year_season[
      seasonal_demand$year %in% c(2015, 2017, 2019, 2021)
    ]
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Migrant Visits - Resident Visits"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```

```{r}
rm(list = ls())
gc()

library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Open full parquet file
# ------------------------------------------------------------

master_ds <- open_dataset(
  "C:/Users/laswa/Downloads/master_data_with_travel_cost.parquet"
)

names(master_ds)

# First 50 rows
master_50 <- master_ds %>%
  head(50) %>%
  collect()

View(master_50)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Seasonal demand: full data summarized by year-season
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_demand <- master_ds %>%
  filter(!is.na(year), !is.na(season)) %>%
  group_by(year, season) %>%
  summarise(
    total_visits = n(),
    migrant_visits = sum(migrant_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    resident_visits = sum(resident_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_migrant_richness = mean(migrant_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_resident_richness = mean(resident_richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(
    season = factor(
      season,
      levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Fall")
    ),
    year_season = paste(year, season, sep = "-")
  ) %>%
  arrange(year, season)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Figure 1: total visits by season over time
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot(seasonal_demand,
       aes(x = year_season, y = total_visits, group = 1)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Number of User Visits"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Figure 2: migrant vs resident visits by season over time
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_visits_long <- seasonal_demand %>%
  select(year, season, year_season, migrant_visits, resident_visits) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(migrant_visits, resident_visits),
    names_to = "bird_type",
    values_to = "visits"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    bird_type = recode(
      bird_type,
      migrant_visits = "Migrant",
      resident_visits = "Resident"
    )
  )

ggplot(seasonal_visits_long,
       aes(x = year_season, y = visits,
           color = bird_type, group = bird_type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Number of User Visits",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Paper-style figure with Total + Migrant + Resident
# ------------------------------------------------------------

total_visits_long <- seasonal_demand %>%
  select(year, season, year_season, total_visits) %>%
  rename(visits = total_visits) %>%
  mutate(bird_type = "Total")

seasonal_visits_total_long <- bind_rows(
  seasonal_visits_long,
  total_visits_long
)

ggplot(seasonal_visits_total_long,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = visits,
           color = bird_type,
           group = bird_type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = seasonal_demand$year_season[
      seasonal_demand$year %in% c(2015, 2017, 2019, 2021)
    ]
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Number of User Visits",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Figure 4: difference between migrant and resident visits
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_difference <- seasonal_demand %>%
  mutate(
    migrant_resident_difference = migrant_visits - resident_visits
  )

ggplot(seasonal_difference,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = migrant_resident_difference,
           group = 1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = seasonal_demand$year_season[
      seasonal_demand$year %in% c(2015, 2017, 2019, 2021)
    ]
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Migrant Visits - Resident Visits"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Figure 5: migrant share of visits
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_share <- seasonal_demand %>%
  mutate(
    migrant_share_total = migrant_visits / total_visits,
    migrant_share_migrant_resident =
      migrant_visits / (migrant_visits + resident_visits)
  )

seasonal_share_long <- seasonal_share %>%
  select(
    year,
    season,
    year_season,
    migrant_share_total,
    migrant_share_migrant_resident
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(migrant_share_total, migrant_share_migrant_resident),
    names_to = "share_type",
    values_to = "share"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    share_type = recode(
      share_type,
      migrant_share_total = "Migrant / Total Visits",
      migrant_share_migrant_resident = "Migrant / (Migrant + Resident)"
    )
  )

ggplot(seasonal_share_long,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = share,
           color = share_type,
           group = share_type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = seasonal_demand$year_season[
      seasonal_demand$year %in% c(2015, 2017, 2019, 2021)
    ]
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Migrant Share of Visits",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```
```{r}
rm(list = ls())
gc()

library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Open full parquet file
# ------------------------------------------------------------

master_ds <- open_dataset(
  "C:/Users/laswa/Downloads/master_data_with_travel_cost.parquet"
)

names(master_ds)

# First 50 rows
master_50 <- master_ds %>%
  head(50) %>%
  collect()

View(master_50)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Seasonal demand summary
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_demand <- master_ds %>%
  filter(!is.na(year), !is.na(season)) %>%
  group_by(year, season) %>%
  summarise(
    total_visits = n(),
    migrant_visits = sum(migrant_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    resident_visits = sum(resident_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(
    season = factor(
      season,
      levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Fall")
    ),
    year_season = paste(year, season, sep = "-")
  ) %>%
  arrange(year, season)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Common x-axis breaks
# ------------------------------------------------------------

x_breaks <- seasonal_demand$year_season[
  seasonal_demand$year %in% c(2015, 2017, 2019, 2021)
]

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Figure 1: Total, Migrant, and Resident Visits
# ------------------------------------------------------------

visits_long <- seasonal_demand %>%
  select(
    year,
    season,
    year_season,
    total_visits,
    migrant_visits,
    resident_visits
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      total_visits,
      migrant_visits,
      resident_visits
    ),
    names_to = "type",
    values_to = "visits"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    type = recode(
      type,
      total_visits = "Total",
      migrant_visits = "Migrant",
      resident_visits = "Resident"
    )
  )

ggplot(visits_long,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = visits,
           color = type,
           group = type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = x_breaks) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Seasonal Birdwatching Demand",
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Number of User Visits",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      face = "bold",
      size = 14
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Figure 2: Migrant Visit Share
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_share <- seasonal_demand %>%
  mutate(
    migrant_share =
      migrant_visits / (migrant_visits + resident_visits)
  )

ggplot(seasonal_share,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = migrant_share,
           group = 1)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = "#D55E00") +
  geom_point(size = 2, color = "#D55E00") +
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = x_breaks) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Seasonal Migrant Visit Share",
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Migrant Visit Share"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      face = "bold",
      size = 14
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Figure 3: Difference in Visits (Migrant - Resident)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

seasonal_difference <- seasonal_demand %>%
  mutate(
    difference = migrant_visits - resident_visits
  )

ggplot(seasonal_difference,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = difference,
           group = 1)) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  geom_line(
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "#7B3294"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 2,
    color = "#7B3294"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = x_breaks) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Difference in Birdwatching Visits",
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Migrant Visits - Resident Visits"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      face = "bold",
      size = 14
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Average visits per hotspot by year-season
# ------------------------------------------------------------

hotspot_avg <- master_ds %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(cluster_id),
    !is.na(year),
    !is.na(season)
  ) %>%
  group_by(year, season, cluster_id) %>%
  summarise(
    visits = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(year, season) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_visits = mean(visits),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(
    season = factor(
      season,
      levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Fall")
    ),
    year_season = paste(year, season, sep = "-")
  ) %>%
  arrange(year, season)

ggplot(hotspot_avg,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = avg_visits,
           group = 1)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = "#0072B2") +
  geom_point(size = 2, color = "#0072B2") +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = hotspot_avg$year_season[
      hotspot_avg$year %in% c(2015, 2017, 2019, 2021)
    ]
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Birdwatching Visits per Hotspot",
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Average User Visits"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      face = "bold",
      size = 14
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )
```

```{r}
rm(list = ls())
gc()

library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Open full parquet file
# ------------------------------------------------------------

master_ds <- open_dataset(
  "C:/Users/laswa/Downloads/master_data_with_travel_cost.parquet"
)

names(master_ds)

# First 50 rows
#master_50 <- master_ds %>%
#  head(50) %>%
#  collect()

#View(master_50)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Hotspot-season summary
# ------------------------------------------------------------

hotspot_season <- master_ds %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(cluster_id),
    !is.na(year),
    !is.na(season)
  ) %>%
  group_by(cluster_id, year, season) %>%
  summarise(
    total_visits = n(),
    migrant_visits = sum(migrant_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    resident_visits = sum(resident_richness > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  collect() %>%
  mutate(
    season = factor(
      season,
      levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Fall")
    ),
    year_season = paste(year, season, sep = "-"),
    migrant_share =
      migrant_visits / (migrant_visits + resident_visits),
    difference =
      migrant_visits - resident_visits
  ) %>%
  arrange(cluster_id, year, season)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Average across hotspots by year-season
# ------------------------------------------------------------

hotspot_avg <- hotspot_season %>%
  group_by(year, season, year_season) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_total_visits = mean(total_visits, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_migrant_visits = mean(migrant_visits, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_resident_visits = mean(resident_visits, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_migrant_share = mean(migrant_share, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_difference = mean(difference, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(year, season)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Common x-axis breaks
# ------------------------------------------------------------

x_breaks <- hotspot_avg$year_season[
  hotspot_avg$year %in% c(2015, 2017, 2019, 2021)
]

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Figure 1: Average Seasonal Birdwatching Demand per Hotspot
# ------------------------------------------------------------

hotspot_avg_long <- hotspot_avg %>%
  select(
    year,
    season,
    year_season,
    avg_total_visits,
    avg_migrant_visits,
    avg_resident_visits
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      avg_total_visits,
      avg_migrant_visits,
      avg_resident_visits
    ),
    names_to = "type",
    values_to = "visits"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    type = recode(
      type,
      avg_total_visits = "Total",
      avg_migrant_visits = "Migrant",
      avg_resident_visits = "Resident"
    )
  )

ggplot(hotspot_avg_long,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = visits,
           color = type,
           group = type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = x_breaks) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Seasonal Birdwatching Demand per Hotspot",
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Average User Visits per Hotspot",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      face = "bold",
      size = 14
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Figure 2: Average Seasonal Migrant Visit Share per Hotspot
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot(hotspot_avg,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = avg_migrant_share,
           group = 1)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = "#D55E00") +
  geom_point(size = 2, color = "#D55E00") +
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = x_breaks) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Seasonal Migrant Visit Share per Hotspot",
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Average Migrant Visit Share"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      face = "bold",
      size = 14
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Figure 3: Average Difference in Visits per Hotspot
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot(hotspot_avg,
       aes(x = year_season,
           y = avg_difference,
           group = 1)) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  geom_line(
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "#7B3294"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 2,
    color = "#7B3294"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = x_breaks) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Difference in Birdwatching Visits per Hotspot",
    x = "Year-Season",
    y = "Average Migrant Visits - Resident Visits"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      face = "bold",
      size = 14
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )

- Steps: 
        - 1. Limit to the winter months. Classify sites by number of migrant species observations. This creates migrant/resident hotspots.
        - 2. Then create the count of resident and migrant species observations by month for each hotspot. 
        - 3. Then create a line graph for the four groups of hotspot type (resident/migrant) and species observation (resident/migrant).
```
